_note: This file is retained for backward compatibility and documentation. Scenario definitions are now loaded from deployment profiles (deployments/*.json). See deployments/open5gs.json for the active Open5GS scenario configuration.

1.name: scenario_1_netpol
1.description: NetworkPolicy blocks inter-service communication. Services can register but sessions fail.
1.root_cause: A NetworkPolicy blocks ingress traffic between services, preventing session establishment.
1.verification: All expected deployments are ready and sessions can be established.

2.name: scenario_2_configmap
2.description: Deployment references non-existent ConfigMap in envFrom, causing CrashLoopBackOff.
2.root_cause: Deployment has envFrom referencing a non-existent ConfigMap, causing the pod to fail to start.
2.verification: Affected pod restarts and becomes ready. All expected deployments are available.

3.name: scenario_3_scale
3.description: Critical deployment scaled to zero replicas. The service is down; sessions cannot be established.
3.root_cause: Deployment has 0 replicas, so no pod is running.
3.verification: Pod starts and becomes ready. All expected deployments are available.

fallback.description: When LLM remediation fails, the entire deployment is torn down and redeployed from scratch. This ensures a clean state for the next experiment run.

metadata.note: Deployment-specific values (namespace, deployment names, ports) are now defined in deployment profiles under deployments/*.json
